description_long: This is a short, stubby pillar candle with a roughly cylindrical shape and an uneven, organic surface. The candle is a muted beige color, with visible wax drips running down its sides, giving it a well-used and slightly rustic appearance. At the center of the top surface, a small, dark wick is visible, supporting a bright, realistic flame that flickers above the candle. The top edge of the candle is irregular and melted, with some areas appearing more sunken or pooled than others. The wax appears opaque and solid, with a matte finish and subtle textural variations. There is no external holder or base; the candle sits directly on its own wax base.
description: A beige, stubby pillar candle with visible wax drips, an irregular melted top, and a bright flame at its center. The candle has a rustic, well-used appearance and no external holder.
description_view_0: Front view shows the candle's uneven top edge, prominent wax drips, and the flame centered above the visible wick.
description_view_1: This side reveals a large, thick wax drip and a more pronounced bulge at the candle's base, with the flame still visible.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a different set of wax drips and a slightly more indented top surface, with the flame and wick clearly seen.
description_view_3: This view shows a smoother side with fewer wax drips and a more uniform base, while the flame remains central.
